question_text: What is the full registered name of your business?
hint: Use the exact name on your business registration certificate

question_text: What sector does your business operate in?
opt_a: Agriculture & Agro-processing
opt_b: Fisheries & Aquaculture
opt_c: Manufacturing
opt_d: Trade & Retail

question_text: How long has your business been operating?
opt_a: Less than 1 year
opt_b: 1–3 years
opt_c: 4–7 years
opt_d: 8–15 years

question_text: How many full-time employees does your business have?
opt_a: 1–5 employees
opt_b: 6–10 employees
opt_c: 11–25 employees
opt_d: 26–50 employees

question_text: What is your estimated annual revenue (SLL Millions)?
opt_a: Below SLL 50 million
opt_b: SLL 50–200 million
opt_c: SLL 200–500 million
opt_d: SLL 500m–1 billion

question_text: What is the primary purpose of the loan you are seeking?
opt_a: Working capital / cash flow
opt_b: Equipment / machinery purchase
opt_c: Business expansion / new location
opt_d: Stock / inventory purchase

question_text: Does your business have a written business plan or growth strategy?
hint: Formal or informal, covering 1–5 years ahead
opt_a: Yes — a formal, detailed written business plan
opt_b: Yes — an informal or brief written plan
opt_c: No — we plan verbally but nothing is written
opt_d: No — we have no business plan

question_text: How clearly defined is your company's mission and vision?
opt_a: Clearly written down and communicated to all staff
opt_b: We have a mission/vision but it is not widely known
opt_c: We have an informal idea but nothing written
opt_d: We have not defined a mission or vision

question_text: How are major business decisions made in your company?
hint: This helps assess key-man risk — whether the business depends on one person
opt_a: Decisions shared across a formal management team
opt_b: One main person decides but consults the team regularly
opt_c: One main person makes most decisions with little consultation
opt_d: One person makes all decisions alone

question_text: Does your management team use digital tools in day-to-day operations?
opt_a: Yes — emails, cloud storage, project management and video tools
opt_b: Yes — mainly email and WhatsApp for business
opt_c: Partially — some staff use digital tools, others do not
opt_d: No — we do not use digital tools for management

question_text: Is your business fully registered with a valid business license?
opt_a: Yes — fully registered as an LLC or similar with a valid license
opt_b: Yes — registered but license is expired or pending renewal
opt_c: Partially — registration is in progress
opt_d: No — the business is not formally registered

question_text: Has your business been profitable in the last two years?
hint: If COVID significantly affected your business reflect on the two years before that period
opt_a: Yes — profitable in both of the last two years
opt_b: Yes — profitable in one of the last two years
opt_c: We broke even — neither profit nor loss
opt_d: No — we have been operating at a loss

question_text: Overall management capability — which description best fits your business?
hint: Rate 1–5 based on current management team capability
opt_a: 1 — Managerial workforce with limited skills and no clear vision
opt_b: 2 — Limited skills but some understanding of vision and growth plans
opt_c: 3 — Some skills, understands vision, may need support to implement
opt_d: 4 — Strong team, articulates vision, limited support needed
opt_e: 5 — Excellent team with full capability to implement growth plans independently

question_text: Are your production or service delivery levels running above 75% of your capacity?
hint: i.e. you are using most of your available equipment, staff time, and facilities
opt_a: Yes — consistently operating above 75% capacity
opt_b: Around 50–75% of capacity
opt_c: Below 50% of capacity
opt_d: We have never measured this

question_text: Does your business have a quality management or quality assurance system?
hint: Includes any documented processes to check and maintain quality of products/services
opt_a: Yes — a formal QA system with written documentation and regular checks
opt_b: Yes — informal quality checks that staff follow regularly
opt_c: We check quality occasionally but have no system
opt_d: No quality management system is in place

question_text: Does your business have a documented sales and marketing strategy?
opt_a: Yes — a written strategy covering multiple sales channels and marketing activities
opt_b: Yes — a basic written plan for sales
opt_c: We have an approach but it is not written down
opt_d: No — we have no defined sales strategy

question_text: Does your business track competitor activity and market trends regularly?
opt_a: Yes — formal market research conducted regularly and documented
opt_b: Yes — we monitor competitors and markets occasionally
opt_c: We are aware of competitors but do not track them formally
opt_d: No — we do not track competitors or market trends

question_text: Does your business have a reliable supply chain and supplier relationships?
hint: i.e. you have consistent suppliers and can maintain production without major disruptions
opt_a: Yes — reliable, long-term supplier relationships with backup options
opt_b: Yes — reliable suppliers but no backup options if they fail
opt_c: Somewhat reliable — occasional supply disruptions occur
opt_d: No — supply is inconsistent and unpredictable

question_text: Does your business have a working capital facility or cash reserve?
hint: i.e. funds available to cover day-to-day operations even if customers are slow to pay
opt_a: Yes — a formal working capital facility from a financial institution
opt_b: Yes — we maintain a cash reserve from profits
opt_c: Occasionally — cash flow is tight but we manage
opt_d: No — we often struggle with cash flow between sales

question_text: Does your business sell beyond your immediate local area?
opt_a: Yes — we export to international markets
opt_b: Yes — we sell nationally across Sierra Leone
opt_c: Yes — we sell in multiple cities or districts
opt_d: No — we sell only in our immediate local area

question_text: Overall operational capacity — which description best fits your business?
hint: Rate 1–5 based on your operational setup
opt_a: 1 — Limited understanding of operating capacity, no cost structure, no technology
opt_b: 2 — General understanding of capacity, limited ability to improve, MS Word only
opt_c: 3 — Understands capacity and processes, cost structure known, uses MS Excel
opt_d: 4 — Good understanding, has plan to improve productivity, uses advanced tools
opt_e: 5 — Clear plan for operations, logistics and inventory, advanced technology used

question_text: How does your business keep its financial records?
hint: ⚠ This is the single most important question for your lendability score
opt_a: We use digital accounting software (e.g. QuickBooks, Wave, Sage)
opt_b: We use Microsoft Excel or Google Sheets
opt_c: We record finances manually in a book or register
opt_d: We do not keep formal financial records

question_text: Does your business prepare monthly or quarterly management accounts?
hint: Management accounts show income, expenses, profit/loss and cash flow for a period
opt_a: Yes — formal management accounts prepared every month
opt_b: Yes — every quarter
opt_c: Occasionally — but not on a regular schedule
opt_d: No — we have never prepared management accounts

question_text: Have you ever successfully accessed a loan or financial product from a formal institution?
hint: Includes bank loans, microfinance, leasing, trade finance, or development finance
opt_a: Yes — successfully repaid a loan or currently repaying one
opt_b: Yes — accessed finance but had difficulties repaying
opt_c: Applied but was not successful
opt_d: Never applied for formal finance

question_text: Are your personal finances completely separate from your business finances?
hint: This is a critical corporate governance indicator for lenders
opt_a: Yes — completely separate bank accounts and no mixing of funds
opt_b: Mostly separate — occasional personal use of business funds
opt_c: Partially — we are working on separating them
opt_d: No — personal and business money is mixed together

question_text: Does your business have a written HR policy or human resource strategy?
opt_a: Yes — a written HR policy that is actively implemented
opt_b: Yes — a written policy but not consistently followed
opt_c: No — we manage HR informally based on common sense
opt_d: No — we have no HR practices in place

question_text: Does your business have a functioning payroll system for employees?
opt_a: Yes — a digital payroll system that processes salaries automatically
opt_b: Yes — a manual payroll that is calculated and documented each month
opt_c: We pay staff informally without a formal payroll
opt_d: No formal payroll — payment is irregular

question_text: Does your business use any integrated software beyond basic Microsoft Office?
hint: e.g. accounting software, CRM, inventory management, ERP, project management tools
opt_a: Yes — we use multiple integrated software systems for different functions
opt_b: Yes — we use one integrated software tool
opt_c: No — we use only Microsoft Office or Google Workspace
opt_d: No — we do not use any software regularly

question_text: Is your business fully compliant with all relevant regulations?
hint: e.g. NASSIT contributions, NRA tax payments, business license renewals, sector regulations
opt_a: Yes — fully compliant with all regulations and up to date
opt_b: Mostly compliant — one or two minor outstanding issues
opt_c: Partially compliant — working to resolve several gaps
opt_d: No — there are significant compliance issues

question_text: Overall financial management — which description best fits your business?
hint: Rate 1–5 based on current financial management capability
opt_a: 1 — No understanding of financial performance or position. No records kept.
opt_b: 2 — Broad understanding of profit/loss and assets. Some evidence of external funding potential.
opt_c: 3 — Practices some planning and monitoring. No sound financial strategy in place.
opt_d: 4 — Reasonable cost and sales estimation. Monitoring financial resources. Developing funding plan.
opt_e: 5 — Full balance sheet capability. Efficient money management and clear finance strategy.

question_text: What is your biggest business challenge right now?
opt_a: Access to finance / capital
opt_b: Finding and keeping good customers
opt_c: Managing cash flow
opt_d: Hiring and retaining qualified staff

question_text: Have you previously received any Technical Assistance or business training?
opt_a: Yes — formal TA from an international programme (ILO, World Bank, USAID)
opt_b: Yes — training from a local organisation or NGO
opt_c: Yes — informal mentoring or coaching
opt_d: No — this would be my first TA support

question_text: Is there anything else you would like the bank or InnovationSL to know about your business?
hint: Optional — this will be included in your diagnostic report

question_text: How did you hear about this diagnostic?
opt_a: From my bank / relationship manager
opt_b: From InnovationSL directly
opt_c: From a business association or chamber of commerce
opt_d: Social media or online
opt_e: From a colleague or fellow business owner
